element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary boss engagement system returns from first game
Requires vertical awareness; includes I.T.O, Eye Lips, Kanako
Sand worms and Gapurin pit traps introduce environmental threats
New movement constraints; aquatic minions (Shark Fin, Blade Ray, Exploding Urchin)
High-temperature arena; boss Lavicus spawns minion eggs
All previous bosses fought sequentially before final Famardy encounter
Destroy Famardy remnants under time limit for good ending

typical_relief_triggers: Level completion rating screen (Good/Very Good/Nice Going rankings)
Point bonus award (1000-100000 range based on performance)
typical_tension_triggers: Environmental hazard zones (Desert, Lava, Sea Bed)
Multiple minion spawns during level progression
Timed destruction phase on final boss
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Escalating difficulty through environmental progression; moderate pacing in scrolling levels with spike tension at boss encounters and final stage gauntlet

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bonus stages available outside main level progression

difficulty_curve.early_game: American City and French City establish core mechanics with humanoid/manageable bosses (Huge Frogger, Eifflelyte)
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Grand Canyon through Sea Bed introduce environmental complexity (sand traps, aquatic constraints); bosses become more alien and aggressive
difficulty_curve.late_game: Lava Zone and Enemy Hideout emphasize pattern mastery; Lavicus egg-spawning creates pressure; final gauntlet requires endurance across all boss patterns; timed destruction adds execution stress
notable_spikes: Sea Bed (5) - aquatic movement constraints combined with spiked shell boss (Aqua Slug)
Lava Zone (6) - egg-spawning boss adds minion pressure
Enemy Hideout (7) - sequential boss gauntlet drains resources before final encounter
Final boss timed destruction - immediate consequence for failure (bad ending vs good ending)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level introduces a distinct global location with associated minion types and hazards (American City → French City → Grand Canyon → Desert → Sea Bed → Lava Zone → Enemy Hideout). Environment communicates escalating alien threat.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Desert (Level 4) with Sand Worm and Gapurin pit traps; Sea Bed (Level 5) with aquatic enemies

notable_level_patterns.description: Each side-scrolling level terminates in boss battle using button-mashing grapple mechanics inherited from first game. Genesis version inverts this: one-on-one fighting game format with no scrolling levels.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Level 1 boss Huge Frogger through Level 6 boss Lavicus

notable_level_patterns.description: Level 7 (Enemy Hideout) requires players to defeat all 6 previous bosses sequentially before encountering final boss Famardy. Creates endurance test and pattern recognition culmination.
Post-level ranking correlates performance to point bonuses (Good=1000 through Mystery Zone=100000). Provides feedback loop for replayability and skill expression.
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Applied across all 7 levels plus bonus stages

notable_level_patterns.description: Final boss destruction triggers timed minion-destruction phase. Success within time limit = good ending; failure = bad ending. Only ending mechanism in game structure.
